title: pterocarpus indicus f. echinatus
intro: Pterocarpus indicus f. echinatus, commonly known as the Indian kino tree, is a variant of the Pterocarpus indicus tree species, characterized by its distinctive spiny or warty appearance. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pterocarpus indicus f. echinatus include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, wounds, and digestive issues, as well as its potential role in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to respiratory issues.
